jarvis.text: Why did the AI cross the road? To optimize the reward function.

jarvis: I told a joke about UDP. I don't know if you got it.
My favorite kind of humor? Dry. Like my firmware updates.
I would tell you a joke about recursion, but you'd have to understand it first.
Why don't scientists trust atoms? I have the same answer. It's the same as everyone else's.

genx.text: I had to walk uphill both ways to hear a modem scream. We turned that into a personality.

genx: My mixtape had a personality disorder. Side A was sad, Side B was also sad but with synthesizers.
We didn't have streaming. We had a TV and a parent who said 'find something to do'.
I miss the days when the most stressful thing was a busy signal.
My generation invented eye-rolling as a coping mechanism. It still works.
We didn't get participation trophies. We got yelled at and we turned out fine. Mostly.
I have a CD binder and no one to show it to.
I survived dial-up, Y2K, and the Spice Girls. I can survive this.
I owned a pager. I'm not proud, but I'm not ashamed either.
My first crush was a singer on a cassette single. I never learned her last name.

millennial.text: I can't afford to buy a house, but at least my podcast subscription is on autopay.

millennial: My therapist hears more from me than my parents ever did.
I have a degree, three roommates, and a plant I'm emotionally attached to.
I'm not procrastinating, I'm just letting my motivation marinate.
I asked for a participation trophy and now I have one. It did not help.
My retirement plan is 'die with debt' and I'm at peace with that.
I watched a coworker get fired over Slack and I still haven't processed it.
I'm an adult. I just forgot which part.
I bought a book on how to be an adult. It's on my nightstand, unopened, judging me.
I have a savings account. It has $47 in it and a dream.

boomer.text: I walked to school in the snow, uphill both ways, and we liked it. We also liked silence.

boomer: In my day, we had three TV channels and we watched all of them. Out of respect.
I have a flip phone, a landline, and a VCR. Two of those are still in use.
My retirement plan is the pension. I earned it by being bored for forty years.
I don't need a password manager. I have a notebook labeled 'passwords' and a sharpie.
I told my grandkids a story about a rotary phone. They thought I was describing a museum exhibit.
I don't doomscroll. I do one crossword puzzle and call it a day.
The most stressful thing in my life was a wrong number on a party line.
I own a CD, a cassette, a vinyl, and a streaming subscription. I refuse to pick a side.
I have a New Year's resolution every year. It's to keep my New Year's resolution. I am twelve for twelve.

genz.text: It's not a phase, mom. It's a personality I monetized on TikTok.

genz: I can't even. I literally cannot even. I am out of evens.
I have seven side hustles, two podcasts, and zero savings. The vibes are immaculate.
I was diagnosed with a vibe disorder. The treatment is posting about it.
I told my therapist I was burned out. She said 'same'. We high-fived and called it a session.
I identify as financially stable. Please don't check my bank account.
My entire personality is a Spotify wrapped and a Pinterest board. Fight me.
I use the word 'literally' literally correctly. Which is to say, I use it as decoration.
I aged out of TikTok. Now I'm on BeReal pretending I don't miss TikTok.
I have a parasocial relationship with my barista. He knows my order. I don't know his name.

dad.text: I'm on a seafood diet. I see food and I eat it.

dad: I used to hate facial hair, but then it grew on me.
Why don't scientists trust atoms? Because they make up everything.
I told my computer I needed a break, and it said 'no problem, I'll go to sleep'.
I'm reading a book about anti-gravity. It's impossible to put down.
I would tell you a UDP joke, but you might not get it.
Why did the scarecrow win an award? Because he was outstanding in his field.
I used to play piano by ear, but now I use my hands.
What do you call fake spaghetti? An impasta.
Why don't eggs tell jokes? They'd crack each other up.
I told my wife she was drawing her eyebrows too high. She seemed surprised.
I'm so good at sleeping, I can do it with my eyes closed.
What do you call a bear with no teeth? A gummy bear.
Why did the bicycle fall over? Because it was two-tired.
I'm on a whiskey diet. I've lost three days already.
What do you call cheese that isn't yours? Nacho cheese.
Why don't skeletons fight each other? They don't have the guts.
I used to be addicted to the hokey pokey, but I turned myself around.
What do you call a fish wearing a bowtie? Sofishticated.
Why did the math book look so sad? Because it had too many problems.
I asked the librarian if the library had any books on paranoia. She whispered, 'They're right behind you'.
What do you call a factory that makes okay products? A satisfactory.
Why don't oysters donate to charity? Because they're shellfish.
I got fired from the calendar factory. All I did was take a day off.
What do you call a sleeping dinosaur? A dino-snore.
